pattern: Two independent two-pointer passes, one skipping over special characters and one skipping over letters, reverse each group in place using the positions it originally occupied.
hints: The two reversals are independent: letters only ever swap with other letters, and special characters only ever swap with other special characters.
A position's category (letter or special) never changes across the two passes, so you can run each pass directly on the same array without tracking original positions separately.
Use a two-pointer sweep from both ends: for the letters pass, skip special characters and swap the letters you find; then repeat with roles swapped for the specials pass.

constraints.note: lowercase English letters and the characters in "!@#$%^&*()" only

edge_inputs.description: one special character between two letters
alternating letters and specials to test position bookkeeping
specials clustered at both ends around a run of letters

stress.description: random mix of letters and the allowed special characters at max length
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = string.ascii_lowercase + '!@#$%^&*()'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))]
